reasoning: The vulnerable code path (warn-only on md4/md5) is only reachable when a caller explicitly sets a non-default signingScheme. The library defaults to signingScheme='pss' with hash='sha256' (lines 3268, 3278), so the broken-hash branch is never entered under default configuration. To reach the warn-only path, a caller must explicitly pass signingScheme:'pkcs1-md5' or an equivalent object form. The allowlist at line 3328 does not protect against md4/md5 — both are present in NODE_HASHES — but the opt-in nature of the configuration means this requires deliberate misconfiguration. The public API (setOptions / constructor options) is exported and reachable, but gate_breachable is false because exploitation requires explicit non-default caller configuration. Severity is downgraded from medium to low because a developer must actively choose a broken hash; the library does not silently select it.
